claim: GPT-5.2 achieves 52.9% on ARC AGI 2 (up from 17% on 5.1), with 390x efficiency improvement ($4500/task to $11/task vs O3 High from year prior)
actionable: Monitor GPT-5.2 performance on reasoning benchmarks; efficiency gains make complex reasoning accessible to more applications than before

claim: GPT-5.2 visual reasoning error rates cut roughly in half vs 5.1 on chart reasoning and UI understanding (64%->86% on GUI screenshots)
actionable: Consider GPT-5.2 for computer use / UI automation tasks; significant improvements in screenshot understanding enable better agentic desktop interaction

claim: GPT-5.2 shows 2x improvement on tool use benchmarks (TAO-2 customer support 47%->98.7%) and handles longer chains of tool calls vs 5.1
actionable: Test GPT-5.2 for agentic tool-calling workflows; doubling of tool accuracy makes it viable for multi-step agent tasks previously unreliable

claim: GPT-5.2 reduces hallucination rate from 12.7% (5.1) to 6.2%, meaningful for applications requiring factual reliability
actionable: Use GPT-5.2 for hallucination-sensitive tasks (financial calcs, document analysis); 50% reduction in hallucination rate improves safety margin

claim: GPT-5.2 pricing increased significantly ($1.25->$1.75 input, $10->$14 output per million tokens); higher cost offsets by quality gains for reasoning-heavy tasks
actionable: Calculate ROI on GPT-5.2 for your tasks; higher pricing is worth it if task requires reasoning/visual understanding or saves engineer time via better quality

claim: GPT-5.2 achieves 100% accuracy on AIME 2025 (math competition benchmark); state-of-the-art on multiple reasoning benchmarks signals continued scaling benefits
actionable: Use GPT-5.2 for complex math, reasoning, and synthesis tasks; benchmark gains suggest it's frontier model for structured reasoning
